lastAssistantMessage:
Confirmed. The failure is real:

- `x-ai/grok-4-fast:free` now returns `404` because Grok 4 Fast is deprecated.
- `google/gemini-2.5-flash` and `openai/gpt-4o-mini` return `403` because the current OpenRouter key has hit its total limit.
- Current xAI replacements like `x-ai/grok-4.3` and `x-ai/grok-build-0.1` are available, but also blocked by that key limit.

I patched dmux so pane analysis no longer depends on the deprecated Grok model:
- Removed `x-ai/grok-4-fast:free`.
- Kept Gemini/GPT-4o-mini as preferred models.
- Added a second-tier free fallback using tested JSON-capable models: `openai/gpt-oss-120b:free`, then `nvidia/nemotron-3-super-120b-a12b:free`.
- Updated slug/merge OpenRouter fallbacks and docs.
- Added a regression test so the deprecated model does not come back.

Live patched analyzer check succeeded with the current capped key: it returned `{"state":"open_prompt"}`.

Validation:
- `pnpm run typecheck` passed.
- `pnpm exec vitest --run __tests__/paneAnalyzer.test.ts __tests__/paneAnalyzer.snapshot.test.ts` passed.
- A broader `pnpm test -- paneAnalyzer` invocation ran nearly the whole suite and hit one unrelated `paneLifecycle` timeout; the analyzer tests themselves passed.
